requirements.text: The PBDU shall measure the supply input voltage at the main power input terminal.
The PBDU shall measure the output voltage of each controlled power load channel.
The PBDU shall measure the current consumed by each controlled power load channel.
The PBDU shall detect an overcurrent condition on each controlled power load channel.
The PBDU shall detect an undervoltage condition at the main power input terminal.
The PBDU shall detect an overvoltage condition at the main power input terminal.
The PBDU shall provide voltage and current measurement values to the communication interface.
The PBDU shall update voltage and current measurements periodically during normal operation.
The PBDU shall control the activation and deactivation of each power load channel.
The PBDU shall support independent control of each power load channel.
The PBDU shall switch off a power load channel when an overcurrent condition is detected on that channel.
The PBDU shall maintain each power load channel in a safe state during system initialization.
The PBDU shall prevent activation of a power load channel when the input supply voltage is outside the allowed operating range.
The PBDU shall report the commanded state and actual state of each power load channel.
The PBDU shall support diagnostic control commands for power load testing.
The PBDU shall store the last valid commanded state of each load channel when required by the system state manager.
The PBDU shall monitor the internal MCU operating status.
The PBDU shall monitor the internal temperature of the power distribution unit.
The PBDU shall detect loss of communication with external sensors connected to analog inputs when applicable.
The PBDU shall detect short-to-battery faults on controlled power load outputs.
The PBDU shall detect short-to-ground faults on controlled power load outputs.
The PBDU shall detect open-load conditions on supported power load channels.
The PBDU shall store diagnostic trouble information when a monitored fault is detected.
The PBDU shall provide health status information through CAN and Ethernet communication interfaces.
The PBDU shall support CAN communication with external vehicle control units.
The PBDU shall support Ethernet communication with external vehicle control units.
The PBDU shall receive load control commands through the CAN communication interface.
The PBDU shall receive load control commands through the Ethernet communication interface.
The PBDU shall transmit voltage, current, temperature, and diagnostic status information through CAN.
The PBDU shall transmit voltage, current, temperature, and diagnostic status information through Ethernet.
The PBDU shall detect communication timeout conditions for safety-relevant control messages.
The PBDU shall reject communication messages with invalid checksum, counter, or data length information when such protection is configured.
The PBDU shall implement at least the states Off, Initialization, Normal Operation, Degraded Operation, Fault, and Sleep.
The PBDU shall enter Initialization state after power-up.
The PBDU shall enter Normal Operation state when initialization is completed and no blocking fault is present.
The PBDU shall enter Degraded Operation state when a non-critical fault limits system functionality.
The PBDU shall enter Fault state when a critical fault is detected.
The PBDU shall deactivate affected power load channels when entering Fault state.
The PBDU shall enter Sleep state when a valid sleep request is received and wake-up conditions are not active.
The PBDU shall wake from Sleep state when a valid wake-up event is detected.
The PBDU shall operate from a nominal 12 V vehicle electrical supply.
The PBDU shall tolerate transient voltage conditions defined for the vehicle electrical environment.
The PBDU shall protect its power outputs against short-circuit conditions.
The PBDU shall provide reverse polarity protection at the main power input.
The PBDU shall limit leakage current on deactivated power load channels.
The PBDU shall provide electrical isolation or protection between external sensor inputs and internal MCU pins where required.
The PBDU shall support analog sensor input voltage measurement within the configured sensor input range.
The PBDU shall meet electromagnetic compatibility requirements applicable to automotive electronic control units.
The PBDU shall use an automotive-grade MCU suitable for the intended temperature and voltage operating range.
The PBDU shall use CAN communication according to the vehicle network architecture.
The PBDU shall use Ethernet communication according to the vehicle network architecture.
The PBDU shall support the number of power load channels defined by the system architecture.
The PBDU shall fit within the mechanical envelope allocated for the power distribution unit.
The PBDU shall use automotive-qualified connectors for power, sensor, CAN, and Ethernet interfaces.
The PBDU shall comply with the project-defined coding guidelines for embedded software.
The PBDU shall complete system initialization within the project-defined startup time.
The PBDU shall update critical diagnostic information within the project-defined diagnostic monitoring cycle.
The PBDU shall detect and react to critical power output faults within the project-defined fault reaction time.
The PBDU shall maintain availability of unaffected power load channels when a fault is isolated to a single channel.
The PBDU shall support software diagnostics without preventing normal operation of unaffected functions.
The PBDU shall store diagnostic information in non-volatile memory when required by the diagnostic strategy.
The PBDU shall provide deterministic behavior for safety-relevant load control functions.
The PBDU shall support maintainability by providing clear diagnostic identifiers for monitored faults.
Each system requirement shall have a unique identifier.
Each system requirement shall be traceable to at least one stakeholder requirement or system feature.
Each system requirement shall be traceable to at least one verification method.
Each safety-related requirement shall be traceable to the corresponding hazard analysis or safety concept element.
Each cybersecurity-related requirement shall be traceable to the corresponding threat analysis or cybersecurity concept element.
Requirement changes shall be reviewed and approved before implementation.
Requirement status shall be tracked using the project-defined lifecycle states.
Requirement verification results shall be recorded and linked to the verified requirement.
